philosophy: Compare CCPM-with-skill output against a deterministic traditional-CPM baseline (cpm_baseline.py: ASAP, safe durations, no resource leveling, no buffers, calendars ignored). Success = CCPM schedule is resource-feasible (0 overloaded resource-days, calendar-aware where calendar.csv exists) with :PB/:FB-attached buffers, while the CPM Gantt's utilization panel typically shows red over-capacity blocks and work scheduled across grey outage windows. Task inputs use predecessor_ids/resource_ids columns; website-launch also carries url columns that must pass through to schedule.csv. make_review.py renders inputs plus both schedules into a single self-contained <iteration>/review.html for side-by-side comparison.

evals.prompt: Build a CCPM schedule for the website relaunch (tasks.csv safe estimates, resources.csv, calendar.csv availability: dev out days 10-13, editor out days 0-5).
evals.expected_output: Leveled, buffered CCPM schedule that places no dev work on days 10-13 (contiguous tasks, no splitting across the outage); urls pass through to schedule.csv and appear as links in summary.md; gantt utilization panel shows grey unavailable blocks. CPM baseline overloads dev and ignores the calendar.

evals.prompt: CCPM schedule; durations already aggressive (no cut); two sink reports.
evals.expected_output: No double-cut; both sinks buffered. No calendar supplied (tests the optional-input path). CPM baseline happens to be conflict-free (honest negative for the overload contrast).

evals.prompt: CCPM schedule for kitchen renovation, comfortable estimates, 3 trades; calendar.csv gives availability (builder on another job days 8-10, plumber away days 0-2).
evals.expected_output: Builder contention leveled around the days 8-10 outage (no builder task straddles it), Tiling on critical chain via resource link; grey unavailable blocks visible in utilization panel. CPM overloads builder and schedules across the outage.

evals.prompt: CCPM schedule for machine retrofit with heavy mechanical-fitter contention.
evals.expected_output: Fitter conflicts leveled (resource link in chain); no calendar supplied. CPM overloads mech 4 resource-days.
